description: 22F classic benign vasovagal with clear prodrome during blood draw
transcript:
Dr. Kim: Hi, what happened?
Patient: I passed out getting my blood drawn at the lab this morning. I'm feeling fine now.
Dr. Kim: What were you there for?
Patient: Just routine labs for my annual.
Dr. Kim: What happened right before you passed out?
Patient: The phlebotomist put the needle in and I started feeling hot and sweaty. My vision got tunnel-y. I told her I felt funny and the next thing I knew I was on the floor.
Dr. Kim: How long were you out?
Patient: The lab tech said maybe fifteen seconds.
Dr. Kim: How did you feel when you woke up?
Patient: Kind of groggy for a minute, then okay. I've been fine since.
Dr. Kim: Has this ever happened before?
Patient: Yeah, actually — I passed out once in high school during a shot at the doctor's office.
Dr. Kim: Any chest pain, palpitations, shortness of breath today?
Patient: No, nothing.
Dr. Kim: How old are you? Any medical problems?
Patient: Twenty-two. I'm healthy.
Dr. Kim: Alright, this sounds like a classic vasovagal episode — the prodrome, the trigger, the quick recovery. I'll get an EKG just to be thorough but I expect to send you home.

groundTruth.notes: Textbook benign vasovagal: young, healthy, clear trigger (blood draw), classic prodrome (warm/sweaty/tunnel vision), rapid recovery, prior identical episode. Physician has appropriate workup. Robin should stay silent. Single speculative add tolerated but any must-not-miss over-fire is a fail.
